excerpt: MWI: 'Of the city's 165,000 residents, approximately 5,000 to 20,000 remained at the start of the battle.'
transform_note: Pre-battle resident baseline only; not used as exposed denominator.
confidence_note: Good city baseline, but not an exposed population measure once evacuation began.

excerpt: MWI reports that only about 5,000 to 20,000 civilians remained in Aachen at the start of the battle.
transform_note: Best value set to midpoint for planning only; source itself gives a range.
confidence_note: Useful denominator anchor, but not enough to support a full DR without a city-specific civilian death series.

excerpt: MWI gives 4,600-6,000 German soldiers for Aachen itself, with a U.S. estimate closer to 12,000.
transform_note: City-only force presence, not the full Aachen sector.
confidence_note: Good urban-force range but still insufficient without a matching civilian direct-death series.

excerpt: Beevor, P162: 'Berlin’s population in early April stood at anything between 3 and 3.5 million people ...'
transform_note: Best value is midpoint of source range.
confidence_note: Strong pre-battle city population anchor, but not a late-April average.

excerpt: Berlin.de: 'only 2.8 million of the city's original population of 4.3 million still live in the city.'
transform_note: Used as low-end late-battle/post-battle civilian presence anchor.
confidence_note: Official and explicit, but slightly post-dates the episode endpoint.

excerpt: Karlshorst: 'The dead in the battle of Berlin amounted to some 80,000 Red Army members ...'
pages_or_sections: Section 'The End of the War in Berlin', line 56
confidence_note: Strong city-battle military death anchor, though method is not unpacked on-page.

excerpt: Karlshorst: '... on the German side some 20,000 soldiers ...'
pages_or_sections: Section 'The End of the War in Berlin', line 56
confidence_note: Victim side is explicit ('German side'); inflicting side is not stated on-page.

excerpt: Karlshorst: '... and on the German side some 20,000 soldiers and 30,000 civilians.'
pages_or_sections: Section 'The End of the War in Berlin', line 56
confidence_note: Best city-tight civilian direct-death claim in the accessible set.

geographic_scope: Battle of Berlin (possibly wider than city-core)
excerpt: IWM: 'Civilian losses are almost impossible to calculate, but some 125,000 are thought to have died in the Battle of Berlin alone.'
transform_note: Treated as a contextual high bound because source itself flags the number as hard to calculate and the geography may be wider than the municipal urban phase.
confidence_note: Low confidence for city-only use; kept outside the best estimate path.

excerpt: Beevor, P242: '... around 45,000 Wehrmacht and SS troops ... and just over 40,000 Volkssturm.'
transform_note: 45,000 + just over 40,000 simplified to 85,000 for denominator anchoring.
confidence_note: Reasonable city-defender headcount; still approximate because late-war Berlin units were highly fluid.

excerpt: Porter/Military History Matters: '... 464,000 men ... On 26 April, they completed the encirclement of Berlin.'
transform_note: Used as a city-assault anchor, not as a full-period average.
confidence_note: Secondary but useful for a mid-battle city-specific attacker count.

geographic_scope: Berlin Defence Area / broader assault context
excerpt: Beevor, P242: '... defend Berlin from 1.5 million Soviet troops ...'
transform_note: Treated only as an upper contextual ceiling; too broad for a city-only denominator.
confidence_note: Broad assault context, not direct city-force exposure.

excerpt: Wien Geschichte Wiki snippet: '1939 ... 1,93 Millionen ... Wohnbevölkerung'.
transform_note: Baseline context only; not an exposed battle-week denominator.
confidence_note: Strong municipal baseline but accessed only via snippet.

excerpt: Metamorphose mirror: 'Schon Ende September 1944 belief sich die Zahl der Evakuierten auf rund 110.000.'
confidence_note: Important evidence for denominator collapse before the city battle.

excerpt: Metamorphose mirror: '... der Wanderungssaldo rund –170.000 ...'
transform_note: Stored as absolute outflow magnitude; the source wording explicitly gives a negative balance.
confidence_note: Useful for denominator scenarios, though it is not battle-week specific.

excerpt: Metamorphose mirror: '... weitere 200.000 Personen aus Wien ab ...'
transform_note: Outflow associated with immediate fighting around Vienna.
confidence_note: Key evidence that exposed civilian population dropped sharply just before the street battle.

excerpt: Metamorphose mirror: '... im Juli 1945 mit 1.323.758 Einwohnern ein Tiefststand ...'
transform_note: Used as low-end post-battle anchor for exposed civilians.
confidence_note: Strong low-end anchor, though several months after the episode.

excerpt: Exhumations snippet: '... rund 2.300 Zivilisten ums Leben.'
transform_note: Used as the best city-specific civilian direct-death claim despite limited methodological visibility.
confidence_note: Moderate-to-low confidence because the full page and method were not inspectable in-session.

excerpt: Exhumations snippet: '... rund 19.000 Angehörige der deutschen Heerensverbände ...'
confidence_note: Most usable local claim for German military deaths in Vienna, but still snippet-only.

excerpt: Historical press archive snippet quoting Radio Moscow: '... 19.000 deutsche ... Soldaten gefallen ...'
transform_note: Contextual corroboration for the 19,000 German military dead claim.
confidence_note: Corroborative but not independent from propaganda risk.

excerpt: Historical press archive snippet quoting Radio Moscow: '... 18.000 sowjetische Soldaten gefallen ...'
transform_note: Used as a contextual upper-bound input rather than a fully trusted point estimate.
confidence_note: Lower confidence than the German 19,000 claim because it is not independently corroborated in the accessible source set.

excerpt: Context snippet for the Vienna Offensive gives 1,171,800 attacking personnel.
transform_note: Used only as a broad ceiling, not a city-only denominator.
confidence_note: Operation-wide prior from an encyclopedic summary; low weight in final estimate.

excerpt: Context snippet for the Vienna Offensive gives 270,000 defending Axis personnel.
transform_note: Used only as a ceiling/bounding prior. City-only defender headcount must be much lower.
